description_long: This object is a vintage cathode-ray tube (CRT) television with a boxy, rectangular shape and a slightly curved glass screen dominating the front. The frame is primarily metallic gray, with a thick, rounded bezel surrounding the display. To the right of the screen, there is a control panel featuring a large rotary dial for channel selection, a smaller knob likely for volume, and a speaker grille below. The sides and back are smooth and unadorned, constructed from a solid, matte-finished material. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal decorative elements, reflecting mid-20th-century consumer electronics styling. The television appears robust and heavy, suitable for placement on a sturdy surface in an urban environment.
description: A vintage, boxy CRT television with a curved glass screen, metallic gray body, and side control panel featuring dials and a speaker grille.
description_view_0: Front view showing the curved glass screen, thick bezel, control dials, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a smooth, featureless metallic surface with protruding control dials visible at the front edge.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing a plain, unadorned back panel with no visible ports or features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing a smooth, featureless metallic surface with slight protrusion from the control panel at the front.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, rounded, metallic gray bezel with a matte finish and minimal styling.
attribute_control_button_layout: Cluster of rotary dials and knobs on the front right panel
